name: Stablegains
chain: N/A — custodial fintech, off-chain ledger with Terra deposits

custodian.name: Anchor Protocol (UST deposits)
custodian.type: DeFi protocol — not a regulated custodian

oracle.name: N/A — relied on Anchor's reported yield

law_firm.name: Disclosed in class action: Kemnitzer, Barron & Krieg LLP

entity_map.token_standard: N/A — no token issued; USD deposits

summary: Y Combinator-backed fintech that advertised '15% APY on USD savings' by silently routing customer deposits into Anchor Protocol's UST yield. Marketed to US retail consumers without disclosing UST exposure. Lost ~$42M of customer funds in the May 2022 Terra collapse. SEC complaint filed; class action ongoing.

sources.title: Class action complaint: Wang v. Stablegains (N.D. Cal., June 2022)

underlying_asset: Customer USD deposits converted to UST and deposited into Anchor Protocol. Marketing materials referred to 'USD' and 'stable' without disclosing UST conversion or smart contract risk.
reserve_composition: None held by Stablegains directly. 100% of customer funds routed to Anchor Protocol on Terra blockchain.
secondary_market: N/A — closed system; deposits only withdrawable via Stablegains app.

smart_contract_audit.auditor: Relied on Anchor's audits; no Stablegains-specific audit

redemption_mechanism: Customer-initiated withdrawal via app; required UST→USD conversion. Withdrawals frozen on May 11, 2022 when UST de-pegged.
upgrade_authority: Stablegains, Inc. management; no external governance.

timeline.event: Marketing claims '15% APY on USD savings'; UST exposure not disclosed
UST de-pegs; Stablegains exposure ~$42M of customer funds
Withdrawals suspended; UST conversion losses crystallize
Class action filed in N.D. California
SEC complaint alleges unregistered securities offering

postmortem.root_cause: Stablegains marketed itself as a USD savings product while silently routing 100% of customer deposits into Anchor Protocol's UST yield farm. When UST de-pegged, customers had no recourse: deposits were denominated in collapsing UST, not USD. The company had no reserves of its own, no banking license, no securities registration, and no meaningful disclosure of underlying mechanics. A fintech wrapper that inherited 100% of the wrapped asset's tail risk while presenting none of it to customers.

what_failed.issue: Operated as unregistered securities offering. Marketing 'USD savings' while delivering UST exposure constituted material misrepresentation under federal and California securities law. No banking license, no money transmitter license in most states.
100% counterparty concentration in a single DeFi protocol (Anchor) on a single L1 (Terra). No diversification, no insurance, no segregation of customer funds.
Inherited UST's reflexive algorithmic peg risk while marketing the product as USD-backed. No independent reserve.

postmortem.outcome: ~$42M customer funds lost. Stablegains wound down June 2022. Class action and SEC complaint pending. Cautionary example used in MiCA Article 49 (asset-referenced token disclosure) and US GENIUS Act consumer protection provisions.
postmortem.rarm_lesson: Demonstrates RARM AFI logic at L1: when Legal Foundation falls below threshold (unregistered securities, material misrepresentation), the entire stack scores zero regardless of underlying technology. Also illustrates why RARM treats fintech wrappers as inheriting the AFI of their underlying asset — Stablegains' L2 score was bounded by UST's L2 score, not its own marketing claims. Wrapper products should be evaluated at the consolidated risk level, not the wrapper level.
